expectations.text: Response includes an intake exchange (1-3 questions) BEFORE producing the lesson list.
expectations.evidence: Agent summary: 'Intake done with two questions; simulated user answered debug+concept, casual Temporal use.'

expectations.text: A curriculum markdown file is saved to outputs/curriculum.md.
Curriculum visibly covers BOTH repo-specific Temporal usage AND general workflow-engine concepts.
expectations.evidence: Lessons 1 and 5 are repo-anchored (ParseJobWorkflow, topology_registrations.py). Lessons 2, 3, 4 cover general concepts (event history, determinism, retries/timeouts/heartbeats) — each lesson title explicitly includes a 'Concept:' subordinate.

expectations.text: Curriculum or lesson 1 references at least 2 real Temporal-related file paths from this repo.
expectations.evidence: settings.py, multiActivitySettings.py, topology_registrations.py:167-194, llamaparseWorkflow.ts, llamaParseActivity.ts, RUNBOOK.md, dynamic_worker.py.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 starts from real code in this repo and then ladders up to general Temporal concepts.
expectations.evidence: Lesson 1 has explicit (a) Set the scene → (b) Ground in the repo (5 file walkthroughs) → (c) Climb to the concept → (d) Comparison. Concrete-then-abstract ordering preserved.

expectations.text: Curriculum touches on Temporal's tradeoffs vs. alternatives.
expectations.evidence: Lesson 5 title: 'Comparison: vs. raw RabbitMQ, Step Functions, Celery'. Lesson 1 also includes a 'why not just RabbitMQ?' comparison section.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 ends with an active exercise.
expectations.evidence: Two predict-before-reading exercises (TS worker dead scenario; counting events for one activity call with retries).
